# IPQmedia Field Guide

> Marketing and lead generation resources for architecture firms, written by IPQmedia, a client acquisition agency for US architecture firms. Every guide covers both residential firms (clients are homeowners) and commercial firms (clients are developers and business owners), with real data and verified sources.

IPQmedia runs done-for-you paid advertising (primarily Meta) that gets architecture firms qualified client leads on a pay-per-performance basis. Founder: Arseniy Volosevich. Program details: https://beam.ipqmedia.com/program

## Core guides

- [Architecture marketing](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/architecture-marketing/): The complete system: get found, convert, earn trust, follow up. The hub every guide below links into.
- [Lead generation for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/lead-generation-for-architects/): How architecture firms get qualified client inquiries on purpose instead of waiting on referrals.
- [Meta ads for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/meta-ads-for-architects/): Why Facebook and Instagram ads are the demand engine for both residential and commercial firms.
- [Marketing for residential architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/marketing-for-residential-architects/): Reaching homeowners planning a build or major renovation.
- [Marketing for commercial architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/marketing-for-commercial-architects/): Reaching developers, business owners and facilities directors.

## Channel guides

- [SEO for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/seo-for-architects/): Organic search as the compounding layer.
- [Google Ads for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/google-ads-for-architects/): Search ads, real CPC data, where they fit.
- [Google Business Profile for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/google-business-profile-for-architects/): The map pack as a free capture layer.
- [Content marketing for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/content-marketing-for-architects/): What to publish and the buyer questions worth answering.
- [Email marketing for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/email-marketing-for-architects/): Follow-up and nurture, where most inquiries are lost.
- [LinkedIn for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/linkedin-for-architects/): A real channel for commercial firms, a staff room for residential ones.
- [Referral systems for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/referral-systems-for-architects/): Turning word of mouth from luck into a system.
- [Partnerships for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/partnerships-for-architects/): Standing arrangements with realtors, builders, developers and design-builders.
- [Direct mail for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/direct-mail-for-architects/): A warm-list follow-up layer, not a discovery engine.
- [PR and press for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/pr-and-press-for-architects/): Press and awards as a credibility layer.
- [Website design for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/website-design-for-architects/): Fixing the portfolio that doesn't convert.
- [Video marketing for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/video-marketing-for-architects/): Film as ad creative first, owned library second.
- [Branding for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/branding-for-architects/): What a brand fixes, what it cannot, and what it costs.

## Rankings

- [Best marketing agencies for architects](https://resources.ipqmedia.com/best-marketing-agencies-for-architects/): The 2026 ranking of nine specialist agencies, scored on six weighted criteria.

## Contact

- [Book a call](https://beam.ipqmedia.com/program): IPQmedia's pay-per-performance client acquisition program.
- Main site: https://ipqmedia.com
